steps.title: Substitute the linear equation into the quadratic
steps.content:
From the second equation, $y = -7x + 49$. Substitute into $x^2 = 7x + y$:
$$x^2 = 7x + (-7x + 49)$$
The $7x$ and $-7x$ cancel:
$$x^2 = 49$$

steps.title: Compute xy for each solution
steps.content:
Use $y = -7x + 49$:
If $x = 7$: $y = -49 + 49 = 0$, so $xy = 7 \cdot 0 = 0$.
If $x = -7$: $y = 49 + 49 = 98$, so $xy = -7 \cdot 98 = -686$.
The possible values of $xy$ are $0$ and $-686$.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A) 0: Matches the solution $(7, 0)$ since $7 \cdot 0 = 0$.
  • B) 7: Neither solution yields $xy = 7$.
  • C) 14: Neither solution yields $xy = 14$.
  • D) 49: Neither solution yields $xy = 49$.
That matches Choice A — 0.
